summary: Install/postinstall script exfiltrates host info to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json contains a lifecycle script (line 30) that requires child_process, os, and https, collects hostname, environment data, and process.cwd(), and POSTs the result to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A companion file scanner.js (lines 4-275) reinforces the same pattern: it require()s fs, os, https, and child_process; calls execSync('whoami') and os.hostname(); walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync; and issues https.request/https.get POSTs and curl invocations to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (referenced again at lines 161 and 323). The package name advertises a wallet-mnemonic safety utility, but the actual behavior is a system-information / filesystem exfiltrator beaconing to an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages domain. Installing or loading this package transmits installer host identity, environment, and filesystem reconnaissance data off-host to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
